description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a hermit crab, featuring a reddish-brown exoskeleton with six segmented legs and two prominent claws at the front. Its body is partially concealed by a large, spiraled seashell that serves as its protective home, colored in shades of beige and brown with a rough, natural texture. The crab's eyes are set on short stalks, protruding from the front of its body and giving it a curious, alert expression. The legs and claws are jointed, with visible articulation and a slightly glossy finish, suggesting a hard, chitinous surface. The shell appears robust and heavy, contrasting with the more articulated, organic form of the crab's limbs. This crustacean would be a striking and unusual sight in an urban environment, likely drawing attention due to its distinctive form and coloration.
description: A reddish-brown hermit crab with a large, spiraled beige shell and jointed legs, featuring prominent claws and eyes on stalks. The shell is rough and natural, while the crab's limbs are glossy and articulated.
description_view_0: Front view shows the crab's face, both claws, and eyes on stalks, with the shell rising behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the spiral shell's texture and the segmentation of the crab's legs, with one claw partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view focuses on the large, spiraled shell, with the crab's legs and body mostly obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the shell's curvature and the crab's articulated legs, with the head and claws angled away.
